ACCORD ENTRE LE GOUVERNEMENT DE LA REPUBLIQUE FRANCAISE

ET LES GOUVERNEMENTS DU ROYAUME UNI DE GRANDE - BRETAGNE ET

D'IRLANDE DU NORD, DU CANADA, DE L'AUSTRALIE, DE LA NOUVELLE

AU SUJET DES SEPULTURES MILITAIRES DU BRITISH COMMONWEALTH

EN TERRITOIRE FRANCAIS

AGREEMENT BEIWEEN THE GOVERNMENTS OF THE UNITED KINGDOM

OF GREAT BRITAIN AND NORTHERN IRELAND, CANADA, AUSTRALIE, NEW

ZEALAND, THE UNION OF SOUTH AFRICA, INDIA AND PAKISTAN, AND

THE GOVERNMENT OF THE FRENCH REPUBLIC REGARDING BRITISH

I

Lo Gouvernement de la République Françaino d'une part, et les

Conmonnoalth victimes de la Guerre de 1939-1945 et dont les dépoullle:

reposent en territoiro Irangeis, sont contenua des dispositions suivantes :

TITRE I

Toutefola; conformément aux dispositiors de la l01 du Il octobre 1946, 00s torrains demeureront la proprlété de l'Etat Irançais.

The Government of the French Republic of the one part and the

Ireland, Canada, Australia, Ner Zealand, the Union of South Africa,

India and Pakistan (hereinafter referred to as "the Commonwealth countries") of the other part, being ragolved to maintain the principles of an Agreement signed in Paria on 26th November 1918 and entitled "Agreement betteen His Britannie Majesty's Government and the

Gorernment of the French Republle relative to the Graves of British soldlere in Franco", and to extend those principles to the cemeteries, graves and memorials of members of the Armed Forces of the Commonwealth countries who fell in the War of 1939-1945 and whose remains rest 1n

French territory, have agreed as follows:

PARIM I

Article I. - The French Government recognises the Imperial War

Graves Cormission (hereinafter referred to as "the Cormission") which nas Incorporated by Royal Charter dated 21st May 1917, as the sole organisation entrusted on behalf of the Governmenta of the Conmonwealth countries parties hereto with all measures concerned witt the con- struction, laying out and maintenance of the Conmonwealth war Cenet- erles, Graves and Memorials. Consequently, the Commission shall hare the right to act in French territory as a properly constituted aseociation and to settle with the french authoritles sIl questions concerning Har Cemeteries, Graves and Memorials of the Conmonwealth.

Article 2. - The French Government granta to the Cormission, rithout payment and without time-limit the free use of the land in

French territory selected and used either as permanent war cemeterles for the burial of members of the Armed Forces of the Commonwealth countries ho fell 1n the Mars of 1914-1918 or 1939-1945, or for the conetruction of memorale in their honor. Nevertheless, ae lald down
